name: Zahr ibn Qays
title: Ominous Messenger
shortDescription: A commander of the Kufan army who was tasked with transporting Imam Hussain's (AS) holy head and the captives' caravan from Kufa to Yazid's palace in Sham.
fullStory:
Zahr ibn Qays al-Ju'fi was an opportunistic and infiltrated figure. He previously claimed to be a Shia during Amir al-Mu'minin's (AS) caliphate, but when the Umayyads gained power, he joined Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad's court.

During the Karbala event, he was among the commanders of Umar Sa'd's army. After the war ended and the tents were looted, Ibn Ziyad, trusting his cruelty and submission, assigned him one of the most bitter missions in history.

Zahr ibn Qays, along with Shimr and Shabath, was tasked with taking the chained caravan of Ahl al-Bayt (AS) captives, along with the holy head of Imam Hussain (AS) and other martyrs, through long and harsh routes to Damascus (Sham) to Yazid.

Throughout this long journey, he treated the children and women with utmost cruelty, and upon reaching Sham, proudly gave the report of the Imam and his companions' killing to Yazid. Zahr ibn Qays is the symbol of those who sold their religion to power.
quoteArabic: أَبْشِرْ يا أَمِيرَ المُؤْمِنِينَ بِفَتْحِ اللَّهِ وَنَصْرِهِ... جِئْناكَ بِرَأْسِ الحُسَيْنِ...
quote: (His ominous report to Yazid:) Glad tidings to you, O Commander of the Faithful, of God's victory! We have brought you Hussain's head.
